"cvss_v4": {"score": 6.9, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:N/VC:N/VI:N/VA:L/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-776", "name": "Improper Restriction of Recursive Entity References in DTDs ('XML Entity Expansion')"}], "description": "### Impact

An attacker who uses this vulnerability can craft a PDF which leads to large memory usage. This requires parsing the XMP metadata.

### Patches
This has been fixed in [pypdf==6.10.0](https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf/releases/tag/6.10.0).

### Workarounds
If you cannot upgrade yet, consider applying the changes from PR [#3724](https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf/pull/3724).", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00423, "percentile": 0.33715}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-3crg-w4f6-42mx", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-04-10T20:59:36Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-3crg-w4f6-42mx", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-3crg-w4f6-42mx"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-40260"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-04-17T01:17:39Z", "published_at": "2026-04-10T20:59:36Z", "references": ["https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf/security/advisories/GHSA-3crg-w4f6-42mx", "https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf/pull/3724", "https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf/commit/b15a374e5ca648d4878e57c3b2c0551e7f8cc7f8", "https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf/releases/tag/6.10.0", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-40260", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-3crg-w4f6-42mx"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/py-pdf/pypdf/security-advisories/GHSA-3crg-w4f6-42mx", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf", "summary": "pypdf: Manipulated XMP metadata entity declarations can exhaust RAM", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T20:37:33Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-3crg-w4f6-42mx", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "6.10.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "pip", "name": "pypdf"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 6.10.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: pypdf: Manipulated XMP metadata entity declarations can exhaust RAM
